title: TeraWulf's HPC Revenue Tops Bitcoin Mining for First Time as AI Pivot Accelerates
text: TeraWulf's shift marks a fundamental business transformation. For the first time in Q1 2026, high-performance computing (HPC) hosting generated $21 million versus $13 million from Bitcoin mining—representing over 60% of total revenue. The company is transitioning from volatile Bitcoin mining revenue to stable contracted HPC revenue backed by investment-grade counterparties, reducing exposure to Bitcoin's price cycles and mining difficulty fluctuations. TeraWulf is not alone—competitors like Riot Platforms are similarly pivoting toward AI infrastructure, with activist investors pushing for acceleration. TeraWulf has 60 megawatts of HPC capacity operational and plans 72.5 MW of dedicated infrastructure for Abu Dhabi's Core42. CFO Patrick Fleury noted that with more than 50% of first quarter 2026 revenue derived from HPC hosting, the company expects its revenue mix to continue shifting toward stable, contracted HPC hosting revenues. Essentially, TeraWulf is leveraging its existing low-cost power infrastructure and data centers to capture sustained AI demand rather than chase volatile cryptocurrency block rewards—a more defensible long-term business model.
